Robustness clustered data Real-world survival data often exhibit -cluster heterogeneity due multi-site enrollment, matched case-control designs, hierarchical sampling structures. Ignoring clustering can lead biased hazard ratio estimates invalid standard errors. package addresses issue stratified partial likelihoods, allow baseline hazard function vary freely across strata rather imposing common baseline. Flexible cross-validation criteria Model tuning supports broad range cross-validation criteria. what: Transfer Learning for Cox Models via Bregman Divergence
title: Transfer Learning for Cox Models via Bregman Divergence
text: SurvBregDiv package implements Bregman-divergence-based transfer learning framework survival analysis, enabling principled borrowing external information explicitly accommodating population heterogeneity internal study external sources. package supports two primary study designs: full-cohort Cox proportional hazards models nested case–control (NCC) designs based conditional logistic regression sampled risk sets.

title: Transfer Learning for Cox Models via Bregman Divergence
text: Three external data integration modes Depending type external data available, package supports: Individual-level data: full covariate outcome data external cohort accessible, integration performed via weighted pseudo-likelihood framework. Coefficient-level summaries: external regression coefficients 𝛃̃\widetilde{\boldsymbol{\beta}} available, integration proceeds via Kullback–Leibler divergence penalization. Coefficient + curvature summaries: additional positive semidefinite matrix 𝐀\mathbf{} (e.g., information matrix variance–covariance matrix) provided, integration proceeds via Mahalanobis distance penalization. Robustness clustered tied event times data package accounts two structural features common real-world survival data. -cluster heterogeneity arising multi-site enrollment, matched designs, hierarchical sampling accommodated via stratified partial likelihoods, allow baseline hazard vary freely across strata rather imposing common baseline. Tied event times frequently occur follow-recorded coarse time scale daily records, scheduled clinical visits, discretized follow-intervals, handled tie-correction methods. High-dimensional variable selection Penalized estimation ridge, lasso, elastic net penalties supported variable selection shrinkage, tuning parameters selected via cross-validation default. However, cross-validation optimizes predictive performance rather variable selection stability, selected predictor set can vary considerably across repeated runs minor perturbations data. settings reproducible trustworthy shortlist predictors needed, stability selection also available, aggregates variable inclusion frequencies across subsamples identify predictors consistently selected regardless subjects happen training set. Ensemble integration via bagged estimation Besides cross-validated LASSO stability selection, package supports ensemble strategy combines bootstrapping, external-data integration, model aggregation—conceptually similar bootstrap aggregation (bagging). bagged integration approach reduces variance improves predictive robustness compared relying solely single cross-validated integrated LASSO model. Flexible cross-validation criteria Model tuning supports broad range cross-validation criteria. Discrimination- based measures include Harrell’s concordance index (C-index) [@harrell1982evaluating], partial likelihood–based criteria include Verweij & Van Houwelingen (V&VH) loss cross-validated linear predictor loss. full details criterion, see Appendix: CV Criteria. Pathwise solution integration weights select integration weight η\eta governs strength external borrowing, package provides two complementary tuning strategies: () grid search approach based pre-specified sequence candidate values, (ii) adaptive tuning strategy using Bayesian optimization directly explore integration weight space, particularly efficient evaluating candidate computationally expensive.

title: Cox Proportional Hazards Model with Mahalanobis Distance Transfer Learning — cox_MDTL
text: objective function minimizes negative log-partial likelihood plus penalty term: $$P(\beta) = \frac{\eta}{2} (\beta - \beta_{ext})^T Q (\beta - \beta_{ext})$$ : \(\beta_{ext}\) vector external coefficients. \(Q\) weighting matrix (derived vcov). \(\eta\) tuning parameter controlling strength external information. vcov NULL, \(Q\) defaults identity matrix, reducing penalty standard Euclidean distance (Ridge-type shrinkage towards beta).

title: Cross-Validated Conditional Logistic Regression with KL Integration — cv.clogitkl
text: matched case–control problem handled via clogitkl, maps Conditional Logistic Regression Cox model fixed event time uses coxkl_ties core engine. Cross-validation performed stratum level: matched set treated indivisible unit assigned single fold using get_fold_cc. ensures conditional likelihood well-defined within training test split. criteria argument controls CV performance metric: "loss": Average negative conditional log-likelihood held-strata. fold, conditional log-likelihood computed test matched sets using fitted \(\hat\beta\) corresponding training data; fold-wise losses averaged. "AUC": matched-set AUC based within-stratum comparisons. stratum, case score compared control scores, counting concordant/discordant/tied pairs aggregating across strata. Higher AUC indicates better discrimination. "CIndex": Alias "AUC". 1:m matched case–control setting, matched-set AUC equivalent conditional concordance index, computed using path "AUC". "Brier": conditional Brier score based within-stratum softmax probabilities. stratum, probability assigned member via \(\hat p_{si} = \exp(\eta_{si}) / \sum_{j \S_s} \exp(\eta_{sj})\), Brier score mean squared error \((Y_{si} - \hat p_{si})^2\) across observations. Lower Brier indicates better conditional calibration sharpness. returned object structure "cv.coxkl" objects cv.coxkl_ties, facilitating downstream code reuse.

NULL, subjects assumed stratum. beta numeric vector external coefficients (length p). vcov Optional numeric matrix (p x p) representing weighting matrix \(Q\) Mahalanobis penalty. Typically inverse covariance matrix. NULL, defaults identity matrix. etas numeric vector candidate eta values evaluated. lambda Optional user-supplied lambda sequence. NULL, function computes sequence based nlambda. nlambda number lambda values. Default 100. lambda.min.ratio Smallest value lambda, fraction lambda.max. Default depends sample size relative number predictors. nfolds Integer. Number cross-validation folds. Default 5. cv.criteria Character string specifying cross-validation criterion. Choices : "V&VH" (default): Verweij & Van Houwelingen partial likelihood loss. "LinPred": Loss based prognostic performance linear predictor. "CIndex_pooled": Harrell's C-index computed pooling predictions across folds. "CIndex_foldaverage": Harrell's C-index computed within fold averaged. c_index_stratum Optional stratum vector. Required cv.criteria involves stratified C-index calculation model unstratified. message Logical. TRUE, progress messages printed. seed Optional integer. Random seed reproducible fold assignment. ... Additional arguments passed underlying fitting function.

title: Cross-Validation for Cox MDTL with Ridge Regularization — cv.cox_MDTL_ridge
text: object class "cv.cox_MDTL_ridge" containing: best list containing optimal results: best_eta: selected eta value. best_lambda: selected lambda value. best_beta: coefficient vector corresponding optimal parameters. criteria: selection criterion used. integrated_stat.full_results data frame performance metrics combinations eta lambda. integrated_stat.best_per_eta data frame summarizing best lambda performance metric eta. integrated_stat.betahat_best matrix coefficients best lambda eta. criteria selection criterion used. nfolds number folds used.

text: simulated dataset generated 1:M matched case-control studies (Conditional Logistic Regression, CLR). data organized matched sets (strata), exactly one case (y=1) \(m=4\) controls (y=0) per set.

title: Example Data for Conditional Logistic Regression — ExampleData_cc
code: data(ExampleData_cc)

title: Example Data for Conditional Logistic Regression — ExampleData_cc
text: list containing following elements: train list components training CLR model: z Numeric matrix covariates (dimension \(n_{\mathrm{train}}\times 6\)) columns named Z1–Z6. y Binary outcome vector (1=case, 0=control). stratum Integer vector identifying matched set observation (200 unique strata train). test list structure train, used external evaluation (500 unique strata test). beta_external Numeric vector (length 6) CLR coefficients estimated separate external dataset using Z1–Z6.

title: Example Data for Conditional Logistic Regression — ExampleData_cc
text: Data-generating mechanism: Study design: 1:4 matched case-control study (\(m=4\) controls per case). Covariates: 6 variables (Z1–Z6) drawn correlated multivariate normal distribution. True coefficients: \(\beta = (1, -1, 1, -1, 1, -1)\). Set-specific effect: random stratum-specific intercept \(\theta_i \sim N(0, 0.5^2)\) added linear predictor; eliminated CLR conditioning. Outcome generation: Within stratum \(\), single case (y=1) selected probability proportional \(\exp(\theta_i + Z^\top \beta)\). External beta estimation: beta_external obtained fitting clogit separate simulated dataset slightly different true coefficient vector \(\beta_{\mathrm{ext}} = (0.8, -0.8, \dots)\) correlation \(\rho = 0.3\), using "breslow" tie approximation.

title: Example high-dimensional survival data — ExampleData_highdim
text: list following components: train list containing: z data frame dimension \(n_{\mathrm{train}} \times 50\) containing covariates Z1–Z50. status numeric vector event indicators (1=event, 0=censored). time numeric vector observed survival times \(\min(T, C)\). stratum vector stratum labels (equal 1). test list structure train, covariates dimension \(n_{\mathrm{test}} \times 50\). beta_external numeric vector length 50 (named Z1–Z50) containing Cox regression coefficients estimated external dataset using Z1–Z6, zeros Z7–Z50. beta_external.multi1 External coefficient vector additive Gaussian noise applied nonzero entries beta_external, preserving original sparsity pattern. beta_external.multi2 External coefficient vector weak Gaussian noise applied 50 coefficients, yielding dense low-magnitude perturbation. beta_external.multi3 scaled version beta_external uniformly attenuated signal strength. beta_external.multi4 External coefficient vector subset nonzero coefficients beta_external signs randomly flipped. beta_external.multi5 External coefficient vector weakened original signals small number newly introduced weak signals among previously zero coefficients.

text:
Data-generating mechanism: Covariates: 50 covariates true signals Z1–Z6 noise variables Z7–Z50. Z1, Z2: bivariate normal AR(1) correlation \(ho = 0.5\). Z3, Z4: independent Bernoulli(0.5). Z5 \(\sim N(2, 1)\), Z6 \(\sim N(-2, 1)\). Z7–Z50: multivariate normal AR(1) correlation \(ho = 0.5\). True coefficients: \( eta = (0.3, -0.3, 0.3, -0.3, 0.3, -0.3, 0, \ldots, 0)\). Event times: Weibull baseline hazard \(h_0(t) = \lambda 
u t^{
u - 1}\) \(\lambda = 1\) \(
u = 2\). Given linear predictor \(\eta = Z^	op  eta\), event times generated $$T = \left( rac{-\log U}{\lambda e^{\eta}}ight)^{1/
u},     \quad U \sim \mathrm{Unif}(0,1).$$ Censoring: \(C \sim \mathrm{Unif}(0, \mathrm{ub})\), ub tuned achieve target censoring rate (internal: 0.70; external: 0.50). observed time \(\min(T, C)\) event indicator \(\mathbf{1}\{T \le C\}\). External coefficients: Cox regression models Breslow ties fitted external dataset using Z1–Z6. resulting estimates embedded length-50 coefficient vectors, additional variants constructed represent different forms coefficient perturbation distributional shift.

title: Generate a Sequence of Tuning Parameters (eta) — generate_eta

what: Simulate stratified Cox PH survival data with flexible censoring, optional binary covariates, and optional ties — sim.cox.highdim
title: Simulate stratified Cox PH survival data with flexible censoring, optional binary covariates, and optional ties — sim.cox.highdim
text: Internal function generate time--event data stratified Cox proportional hazards model high-dimensional covariates, stratum-specific multiplicative baseline scaling, controllable random censoring. Event times generated inverse transform sampling using baseline cumulative hazard. model $$h(t \mid Z, s) = \gamma_s \, h_0(t)\, \exp(Z^\top \beta),$$ \(\gamma_s\) stratum-specific positive constant, \(h_0(t)\) baseline hazard, \(\beta\) coefficient vector. Supported baseline hazards: "exponential": \(h_0(t)=1\), \(H_0(t)=t\). "weibull": \(h_0(t)=\alpha t\) \(\alpha>0\), \(H_0(t)=\alpha t^2/2\). Censoring implemented $$T_{\mathrm{obs}} = \min(T, C, \texttt{max_time}),$$ \(C\) random censoring time drawn specified distribution calibrated approximately match target censoring rate. Supported random censoring distributions: "uniform": \(C \sim \mathrm{Unif}(0, c_{\max})\). "exponential": \(C \sim \mathrm{Exp}(\lambda_c)\). "weibull": \(C \sim \mathrm{Weibull}(k, \lambda_c)\), shape \(k\) specified censor_weibull_shape. "lognormal": \(C \sim \mathrm{LogNormal}(\mu_c, \sigma_c^2)\), \(\sigma_c\) specified censor_lognormal_sdlog. "none": random censoring; administrative censoring via max_time. Binary covariates can specified index via binary_idx. variables generated using latent Gaussian threshold model achieve desired prevalence approximate within-binary correlation controlled rho_binary. Specifically, \(B\) binary \(P(B=1)=p\), generated \(B = (L > c)\) \(L\) standard normal \(c=\Phi^{-1}(1-p)\). multiple binary covariates requested, latent variables generated jointly equicorrelation rho_binary. controls latent (tetrachoric) correlation; resulting Pearson correlation among binary variables constrained prevalence generally close identical rho_binary. ties=TRUE, observed times discretized ties_levels bins (based sample quantiles) replaced bin midpoints induce tied event/censoring times. Smaller ties_levels produces stronger ties.

Remaining coefficients (n.beta) set 0. beta_idx Integer vector NULL. Indices (1:n.beta) covariates nonzero true coefficients. NULL, defaults 1:length(beta_value_vec) (original behavior). Must length beta_value_vec filtering valid indices. mu_Z Numeric vector NULL. Mean vector continuous covariates. NULL, zero vector used. indices binary_idx, mu_Z ignored. stratum_size Integer numeric vector. Stratum sample sizes. scalar, recycled length n_stratum; vector, must length n_stratum. rho Numeric. Correlation parameter \([0,1)\) equicorrelation covariance structure used generate continuous covariates. censor_rate Numeric. Target random censoring rate \([0,1)\). 0, random censoring applied (administrative censoring via max_time may still apply). censor_type Character. Random censoring distribution. One "uniform", "exponential", "weibull", "lognormal", "none". max_time Numeric. Administrative censoring time. Observed time min(event, censor, max_time). seed Integer NULL. Random seed reproducibility. baseline Character. Baseline hazard type. One "exponential" "weibull". alpha Numeric NULL. Required baseline="weibull". Controls Weibull baseline hazard \(h_0(t)=\alpha t\); must positive finite. Ignored baseline="exponential". censor_weibull_shape Numeric. Shape parameter \(k>0\) Weibull random censoring censor_type="weibull". scale parameter calibrated internally match censor_rate. Ignored otherwise. censor_lognormal_sdlog Numeric. Log-scale standard deviation \(\sigma_c>0\) log-normal random censoring censor_type="lognormal". mean log scale calibrated internally match censor_rate. Ignored otherwise. binary_idx Integer vector. TRUE, discretize observed times induce ties. ties_levels Integer. Number bins used discretize time ties=TRUE. Smaller values yield stronger ties. Must \(\ge 2\).
